title: centaurea dufourii subsp. lagascae
intro: Centaurea dufourii subsp. lagascae, also known as Lagasca's knapweed, is a plant species within the Asteraceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Centaurea dufourii subsp. lagascae are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. This plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Centaurea dufourii subsp. lagascae is used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
